member.office_address: 1318 Longworth House Office Building Washington DC 20515-4318

form_analysis.form_header_text: Congressman Christian Menefee FY 2027 Appropriation Requests
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: If your organization participates in any federally funded programs, please fill out the following form to detail your requests for programmatic funding or bill or report language. Requests must be merit-based and comply with all current House rules. Requests will only be accepted through this form. The deadline for submissions to our office is 5PM on Monday, March 9, 2026. Late submissions will not be accepted. Please email questions or concerns to MenfeeAppropriations@mail.house.gov.

sections.description: Provide information about the submitting organization and the primary contact.

fields.label: Is your organization located or operates primarily in Texas?
Is your organization located in or operates primarily in the 18th District of Texas?
Organization's Contact / Representation in DC - Name
Organization's Contact / Representation in DC - Email
Organization's Contact / Representation in DC - Phone

sections.description: Details regarding the specific appropriations funding or language request.

fields.label: Select the Appropriations Bill
fields.help_text: Choose the relevant subcommittee or bill for this request
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration [Submission Deadline -- April 18]
Commerce, Justice. and Science [Submission Deadline -- TBD]
Energy and Water Development [Submission Deadline -- TBD]
Financial Services and General Government [Submission Deadline -- TBD]
Labor, Health and, Human Services, Education [Submission Deadline -- TBD]
Transportation, Housing and Urban Development [Submission Deadline -- TBD]
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice. and Science [Submission Deadline -- TBD]
fields.rationale: NASA's Science Mission Directorate is funded through the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies subcommittee.

fields.label: Request Type
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic request for a specific funding level ($9 billion) for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Agency
fields.rationale: NASA is the primary agency for the Science Mission Directorate request.

fields.label: Account
fields.rationale: This is the specific account name for the $9 billion request within the NASA budget.

fields.label: If the request includes bill/report language, please provide draft language here
fields.rationale: The request context explicitly states 'N/A' for bill and report language requested.

fields.label: Are other Members of Congress supporting this request and/or leading a letter (Please specify if a Member of Congress is leading a letter)
fields.draft_value: * Representatives Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading a House programmatic support letter for NASA Science.
fields.rationale: The reference values identify Bacon and Chu as the leads for the House letter on this topic.

fields.label: If requesting an individual letter of support, please provide sample language here
fields.draft_value:
* We respectfully request $9 billion for NASA's Science Mission Directorate in the FY2027 CJS appropriations bill.
* This funding is essential to restore stability to the aerospace workforce after the disruptions of FY2026 and to ensure the U.S. maintains its competitive edge against rapidly advancing international programs, particularly China.
* Sustained investment supports critical local research at the University of Houston System and regional aerospace contractors like Firefly Aerospace Inc.
* NASA Science remains a premier driver of American innovation, returning approximately three dollars to the economy for every dollar invested.
fields.rationale: This draft utilizes the specific $9B ask, the workforce stability and China competition talking points, and references local entities from the spending context (University of Houston System and Firefly Aerospace Inc).

fields.label: Request Funding Action
options: Support President's FY 2023 Budget Proposed Level
fields.rationale: The request of $9.000 billion is a 24% increase over the FY2026 enacted level of $7.250 billion.

fields.label: FY2027 Funding Request (the amount you are requesting)
fields.help_text: Specify the dollar amount requested for Fiscal Year 2027
fields.rationale: This is the exact funding ask provided in the asks_config for NASA's Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Provide a detailed explanation of the problem or issue to be addressed through this request
fields.draft_value:
* Severe funding uncertainty in FY2026 led to the loss of thousands of skilled civil servant and contractor positions, threatening decades of American leadership in space exploration.
* China is rapidly expanding its space science capabilities, including lunar sample returns and planned missions to Jupiter, challenging the U.S. role in setting 21st-century exploration norms.
* Current funding levels risk delaying or canceling peer-reviewed missions identified as top priorities by the National Academies' Decadal Surveys.
* Inadequate investment jeopardizes the STEM talent pipeline and the domestic aerospace industrial base that relies on steady NASA Science demand.
fields.rationale: Draws on the 'Advocacy Context' regarding China competition, workforce loss from FY2026, and the importance of Decadal Survey priorities.

fields.label: Describe the constituency and/or industry benefited by this request
fields.draft_value:
* The Houston aerospace industry, including local contractors like Firefly Aerospace Inc ($22.1M award) and Symmetry Energy Solutions LLC, which support critical mission infrastructure.
* Academic and research institutions in TX-18, specifically the University of Houston System, which manages 11 recent NASA grants totaling over $5.8M.
* The broader STEM workforce, including researchers at the University of Texas Health Science Center at Houston working on advanced life sciences and data analysis.
* Local economic interests benefitted by $35.7M in total NASA Science spending in the district across recent fiscal years (FY23-FY25).
fields.rationale: Uses specific district data: Firefly Aerospace ($22.1M), University of Houston System ($5.8M), and the total district spending figure ($35.7M).

fields.label: Organizations supporting your request
fields.draft_value:
* American Astronomical Society (AAS)
* American Geophysical Union (AGU)
fields.rationale: Lists the allied organizations provided in the Congressional Support section of the reference values.

fields.label: Did you submit a request to your representative's office last year?
fields.rationale: This information is specific to the individual submitter and cannot be determined from the provided context.

fields.label: Have you spoken with our staff about this request. If so, Please list Name and Date
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific question regarding personal interactions with congressional staff and cannot be answered using the provided context.

sections.description: Final acknowledgement of the submission terms.

fields.label: Acknowledgement
fields.rationale: This is a standard acknowledgement field required to proceed with the form submission.

top_contracts.description: Venture-class acquisition of dedicated and rideshare (VADR) umbrella task order
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Procuring dedicated and rideshare launch services for future missions.

top_grants.description: We propose a coordinated microstructural  mineralogical  and isotopic study of fgis from reduced cv3 chondrites to better understand their origin and nature
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Analyzing primitive meteorite components for solar system history.

top_grants.description: eo14042 new observing strategies for beach and dune topography and implications for coastal flood risk
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Mapping beach and dune topography to assess coastal flood risks.

top_grants.description: Biogenic nonmethane volatile organic compounds (BVOCS) emitted by terrestrial ecosystems are of great importance to air quality  tropospheric chemistry  and climate due to their effects on atmospheric oxidants and aerosols. Yet  because of the inherent complexity of these emissions  they remain a highly uncertain component in our predictive capacity of air quality and chemistryclimate interactions. While substantial progress has been made to improve the community S ability to predict bvoc emissions under unstressed conditions  there has been relatively little effort to characterize bvoc emissions under stressed conditions  such as drought and heatwaves  to which the response of bvoc emissions can be substantially different from model predictions  such as by megan2.1  in the absence of algorithms that can accurately represent the processes controlling these emissions under stress conditions.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Predicting air quality changes from stressed ecosystem emissions.

top_grants.description: eo14042 knowledge transfer for robust geoai across space  sensors and time via active deep learning
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Transferring deep learning expertise for robust geospatial data analysis.

top_grants.description: Execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress.   Participate as a key partner and enabler in the age
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Aligning science program execution with national policy guidance

top_grants.description: e014042-characterizing expected uncertainty in spaceborne high-resolution terrain models for examining surface processes
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Validating accuracy of orbital 3D maps for surface analysis.

top_grants.description: Science objectives: the strong zonal winds on the giant planets (i.E.  Jupiter  Saturn  uranus  and Neptune) are one of the most amazing phenomena in our solar system. Such zonal winds set the basic environment for other dynamical processes in planetary atmospheres of the giant planets  such as storms  vortices  and waves. Investigations of the wind fields not only help us better understand the amazing phenomenon but also provide key information about the dynamical environment in which major dynamic processes form and evolve.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Modeling powerful atmospheric jet streams shaping giant planet weather systems.
